---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
    css: styles.css
---

<div style="text-align: center; margin-bottom: 2em;">
  <img src="images/logo.png" width="300">
  <p style="margin-top: 0.5em; font-size: 0.7em">
    Political Elites and Regime Change in the Middle East and North Africa
  </p>
</div>

---

## Project Overview

**MENA-PERC** is a research project funded by an ERC Consolidator Grant (Grant Agreement No. 101044015), based at the [Sant'Anna School of Advanced Studies](https://www.santannapisa.it/en) in Pisa. The project examines the structure, behavior, and transformation of political elites across the Middle East and North Africa, with a particular focus on Egypt, Tunisia, and Turkey over the past century.

Combining large-scale data collection with computational text analysis, MENA-PERC investigates how elite coalitions evolve, how power is distributed within regimes, and how these dynamics shape processes of regime stability, crisis, and authoritarian transformation.

---

## Research Focus

The project centers on three core questions:

- When do political elites choose exclusion over accommodation?
- How do elite coalitions emerge, and how are they structured and maintained in authoritarian regimes?  
- Under what conditions do elites support or resist regime change, including autocratization and democratization?  

Methodologically, the project integrates:

- systematic interview data from extensive fieldwork
- original datasets on political appointments, careers, and elite networks  
- legislative speech corpora and text-as-data approaches  
- computational methods for scaling political positions  

---

## Resources

<div class="grid">
<div class="g-col-4">

### Data  
Datasets on political elites and legislative behavior  

[Access data →](./data/)

</div>

<div class="g-col-4">

### Code  
Data processing pipelines and replication material  

[View code →](./code/)

</div>

<div class="g-col-4">

### Papers  
Publications and working papers  

[Read papers →](./papers/)

</div>
</div>

---

## Team

**Principal Investigator**  
Kevin Koehler — Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna  

**Research Team**  
Camille Abescat — Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna  
Théo Blanc — Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna  
Gennadii Iakovlev — Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna  
Berker Kavasoglu — Heidelberg University  
Lorenzo Fruganti — Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna / Scuola Normale Superiore  
Julius Strunk — Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna / Scuola Normale Superiore  

---

## Contact

For questions, collaborations, or data access requests:

**Kevin Koehler**  
Scuola Superiore Sant'Anna  
📧 [kevin.koehler@santannapisa.it](mailto:kevin.koehler@santannapisa.it)
